description: Wizard-distributed machine-readable contract for the inherited operating-principle corpus pack. The pack is a self-contained set of operating principles and disciplines the generator emits into operator-project scaffolds. Each cell declares applicability (system shape / lifecycle phase / authority gate / data-risk gate), how it is realized (a canonical principle body, an installed scaffold template, or a delegated control), its canonical home + body for principle-bearing cells, per-target operational hooks, and a provisional authority stamp. The pack is self-contained and operator-facing: it carries operator-relevant content and neutral identifiers only. Per-shape adaptation is handled by render-variant selection at emission time, not by logic in the loader.
invariants: C1: all required_pack_fields present; contract_id == 'inherited-corpus'; contract_version == 'inherited-corpus-v1'; corpus_pack_version is a non-empty string; cells is a non-empty list
C2: every cell has all required_cell_fields; every enum-valued field resolves to its closed-set member (fail-closed on unknown value)
C3: cell_id is unique across the pack

C4: realization == 'corpus-body' requires canonical present with a non-empty body AND a non-empty home AND a category; realization in {scaffold-template, delegated} carries no canonical (the body is the installed scaffold template or the delegated control, not pack content)

C5: authority_gate == 'applies-all' requires authority_basis_default == 'not_applicable' AND authority_source_default == 'not_applicable'; authority_gate != 'applies-all' requires authority_basis_default in {provisional_default, operator-profile-derived} AND authority_source_default in {delegated, wizard-default, hard-control, operator-configured}
C6: every target_hooks[] entry has a non-empty target and a hook_type in enums.hook_type
C7: every cell shape_scope[] is a member of enums.system_shape and phase_scope[] is a member of enums.lifecycle_phase
